| Comments |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| Overwrite the planned training days by inserting your achieved reps, weight and sets. Use the notation [weight/band tension/set-up]x[reps in set 1], [reps in set 2], [reps in set 3], e.g. 3' green band x8,7,6 could mean 3 sets were done, with 8, 7 and 6 reps per set in order, and you used a green band at a 3' distance as the resistance. You don't have to note down warm-up sets. |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| If you return the filled in program to me when you have completed all of your planned days, I can plan in the next mesocycle(s). |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| a) and b) denote paired sets. These are like supersets, except you rest in between exercises. Always rest until you feel fully recovered. E.g. 2a) push-up 2b) leg curl would mean you perform a set of push-ups, rest, perform a set of leg curls, rest, etc. |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| If you cannot perform the exercises in pairs for whatever reason, do them consecutively. It is also possible to perform the first sets of the paired exercises in pairs and then perform the later sets as a mixture of paired and consecutive sets. |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| The important thing is to be consistent in the order in which you perform the first sets of the exercises. |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| *19 lbs and 13 lbs resistance (Bodylastics type bands). |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| **Note: Whenever you perform more than 12 reps during any set, you can take the set to complete failure. |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
